intent: Implement workflow phases as explicit execution contracts across template authoring, runtime prompts, recommended skill bindings, dependency-aware sharing, lifecycle/completion state, desktop UI, compatibility, and validation.

complexity.reason: Touches server workflow services, APIs, SkillTool permission boundary, skill catalog/resolver, desktop workflow UI, persistence compatibility, and verification gates.

source_of_truth.evidence_rule: Live repository reads and tests prove implementation facts; project cognition is advisory navigation only.

must_preserve.claim: Workflows are phase execution contracts, not cosmetic editor grouping or skill hints.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Contract semantics are reduced to editor-only layout.

must_preserve.claim: Feature boundary remains unified across fields, constraints, skills, sharing, lifecycle, UI, compatibility, and validation.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Lifecycle or UI controls are split away and lose consistency.

must_preserve.claim: Grouped intent, contract, evidencePolicy, and session-owned runtimeState are preserved.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Runtime state becomes editable template data.

must_preserve.claim: Guidance, policy, evidence, and gate strengths remain distinct.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Every instruction becomes a hard machine gate.

must_preserve.claim: Recommended phase skills bind to existing skills and remain soft by default.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Plugins become primary target or recommended skills auto-run.

must_preserve.claim: Soft audit is bounded to used or clearly relevant skipped/unavailable skills.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Audit lists every recommended skill mechanically or records nothing.

must_preserve.claim: Workflow sharing exports template plus dependency manifest, not bundled skill packages.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Import silently drops missing references or export implies bundled skills.

must_preserve.claim: Lifecycle status stays separate from completion submission outcome.
Pending confirmation uses Confirm, Reject, Retry and blocks duplicate ready submissions.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Pending ready submissions can overwrite silently.

must_preserve.claim: Runtime controls stay safe and authority labels remain explicit.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: UI mixes manual completion with pending confirmation.

must_preserve.claim: Compatibility-first field adaptation comes before grouped persistence migration.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Destructive migration lands without old-template fixtures.

must_preserve.claim: Scheduler, auto-execution, default required gates, skill bundle export, and destructive migration remain out of scope.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Scope expands into execution engine or capability system.

must_preserve.claim: Live repository evidence remains authoritative while project cognition is partial for this area.
must_preserve.stop_and_reopen: Plan claims completion from project cognition alone.

capabilities.name: Recommended phase skill bindings and audit

invariants: Templates own authorable contract fields; sessions own runtime state.
Recommended skills do not bypass SkillTool permissions or auto-execute.
Import/export carries dependency diagnostics, not skill package contents.
Blocked and unable are recoverable completion outcomes.
Pending confirmation blocks duplicate ready submissions and exposes only Confirm, Reject, and Retry.
Unknown fields are preserved unless validation rejects them.
Completion claims require same-area tests and quality gate evidence.
allowed_optimization_scope: Reduce duplication by reusing existing helper APIs.
Add narrow compatibility projection helpers when they clarify grouped semantics.
Add focused fixtures and tests before implementation changes.
Improve UI labels/layout within existing workflow component patterns.
forbidden_scope: Bundling arbitrary skill contents in exports

consequence_obligations.claim: Define stable skill binding identity across local, bundled, plugin, managed, and MCP sources.
Define priority without overriding safety boundaries.

consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen: Missing skills silently degrade to prose.

consequence_obligations.claim: Preserve skill provenance in session lifecycle states.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen: Source edits silently change active session.

consequence_obligations.claim: Make skill use, skip, and unavailable decisions observable when relevant.
consequence_obligations.stop_and_reopen: Skills can run or be skipped invisibly.

consequence_obligations.claim: Validate skill bindings across authoring, import, and start paths.
Define UI labels and affordances for contract states.
Preserve evidence through compaction, resume, report, and imports.
Prevent recursive or unbounded nested invocation.

coverage_gaps.gap: Exact runtime skill audit storage and final report field mapping require implementation classification.
coverage_gaps.routing_decision: continue with explicit task and tests

coverage_gaps.gap: Exact grouped editor copy/layout is implementation-level but semantics are locked.
Current project cognition does not fully encode workflow runtime/editor ownership.
coverage_gaps.routing_decision: continue with live evidence during planning
